complexity.justification: Sequential input processing with indexed output, PERFORM loop, and error handling blocks

annotation_metadata.note: The prior 'CORRECTED' file fixed the missing 'name' field (P1) but still contained fabricated content not present in VSCBEX01.cbl: paragraph names BEGIN_JOB/PROCESS_LOOP/INSERT_RECORD/END_JOB/READ_INPUT/WRITE_OUTPUT do not exist (real names are 1000-Begin-Job, 2000-Process, 2100-Insert-CUSTFile, 3000-End-Job, 5000-Read-LOADFile, 6000-Write-CUSTFile); a VALIDATE_RECORD paragraph and a 'NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD' read clause do not exist; an 'INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR' write clause and HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph do not exist. This file replaces those with verified content and correct line numbers.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX01
* Original author: David Stagowki
* Description: Example 01: Indexed File Processing: Load File
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 22
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
structures.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
structures.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

raw.name: MAINLINE
raw.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
raw.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
raw.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

raw.name: 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
raw.description: Inserts a record into the CUSTFile.

raw.name: BEGIN_JOB
raw.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
raw.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
raw.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

raw.name: 3000-End-Job
raw.description: Closes files and displays end-of-job messages.

raw.name: INSERT_RECORD
raw.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
raw.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
raw.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

raw.name: 5000-Read-LOADFile
raw.description: Reads a record from the LOADFile.

raw.name: END_JOB
raw.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
raw.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
raw.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

raw.name: 6000-Write-CUSTFile
raw.description: Writes a record to the CUSTFile.

raw.name: READ_LOADFILE
raw.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
raw.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

raw.name: IDENTIFICATION
raw.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
raw.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
raw.description: Program identification

raw.name: PROCESS_LOOP
raw.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
raw.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

raw.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
raw.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
raw.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

complexity.justification: Sequential input processing with indexed output, PERFORM loop, and error handling blocks

annotation_metadata.note: The prior 'CORRECTED' file fixed the missing 'name' field (P1) but still contained fabricated content not present in VSCBEX01.cbl: paragraph names BEGIN_JOB/PROCESS_LOOP/INSERT_RECORD/END_JOB/READ_INPUT/WRITE_OUTPUT do not exist (real names are 1000-Begin-Job, 2000-Process, 2100-Insert-CUSTFile, 3000-End-Job, 5000-Read-LOADFile, 6000-Write-CUSTFile); a VALIDATE_RECORD paragraph and a 'NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD' read clause do not exist; an 'INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR' write clause and HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph do not exist. This file replaces those with verified content and correct line numbers.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX01
* Original author: David Stagowki
* Description: Example 01: Indexed File Processing: Load File
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 22
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
structures.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

complexity.justification: Sequential input processing with indexed output, PERFORM loop, and error handling blocks

annotation_metadata.note: The prior 'CORRECTED' file fixed the missing 'name' field (P1) but still contained fabricated content not present in VSCBEX01.cbl: paragraph names BEGIN_JOB/PROCESS_LOOP/INSERT_RECORD/END_JOB/READ_INPUT/WRITE_OUTPUT do not exist (real names are 1000-Begin-Job, 2000-Process, 2100-Insert-CUSTFile, 3000-End-Job, 5000-Read-LOADFile, 6000-Write-CUSTFile); a VALIDATE_RECORD paragraph and a 'NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD' read clause do not exist; an 'INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR' write clause and HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph do not exist. This file replaces those with verified content and correct line numbers.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX01
* Original author: David Stagowki
* Description: Example 01: Indexed File Processing: Load File
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 22
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
structures.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX01
* Original author: David Stagowki
* Description: Example 01: Indexed File Processing: Load File
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 22
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
structures.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

raw.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
raw.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
raw.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

raw.name: PROCESS_LOOP
raw.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
raw.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

raw.name: INSERT_RECORD
raw.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
raw.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
raw.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

raw.name: END_JOB
raw.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
raw.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
raw.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

raw.name: READ_LOADFILE
raw.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
raw.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

raw.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
raw.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
raw.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.

structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.source_snippet:

FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX01
* Original author: David Stagowki
* Description: Example 01: Indexed File Processing: Load File
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 22
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.source_snippet:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID. VSCBEX01.
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers
structures.description: Program identification

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT LOADFile
ASSIGN TO "../data/customer.dat.txt"
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-LOADFile-Status.

SELECT CUSTFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS CUSTFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / two SELECT clauses are explicit and unambiguous
structures.description: Declares two files: LOADFile (line-sequential flat file input) and CUSTFile (indexed customer file, sequential access, for output)

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  LOADFile
    LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
    RECORDING MODE IS F
    BLOCK CONTAINS 0 RECORDS.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==LOADFile==.

FD  CUSTFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==CUSTFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: Record layouts for both files (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage for both files, two counters (records read / records inserted), and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry
structures.description: Program entry point; straight-line perform of begin/process/end phases

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN statements plus a priming read are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens LOADFile for input and CUSTFile for output, then performs the priming read that the loop in 2000-Process relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_LOOP
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
structures.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
structures.description: Copies the current input record into the output record area, then performs the write paragraph to persist it

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
structures.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit; there is no INTO clause and no NOT AT END branch (correcting the prior annotation)
structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.

       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.

structures.name: INSERT_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt

structures.name: READ_LOADFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-LOADFile.
    READ LOADFile
       AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.

structures.description: Reads the next flat-file record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no separate validation paragraph invoked here.

structures.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
structures.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.

FD  CUSTFile.

raw.name: PROCESS_LOOP
raw.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL...END-PERFORM is explicit loop syntax; the guarding IF WS-LOADFile-Good is also explicit
raw.description: Main processing loop, guarded by a check that the priming read succeeded; each iteration inserts the current record then reads the next one (insert-then-read order, since the first read already happened in Begin-Job)

raw.name: END_JOB
raw.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
    DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt

    CLOSE LOADFile
          CUSTFile.
D    DISPLAY "CUSTFile Close Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status.
raw.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup phase
raw.description: Displays end-of-job banner and read/insert counts, closes both files (a debug-only DISPLAY of the close status follows)

raw.name: WRITE_CUSTFILE
raw.source_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile"
       DISPLAY "Write CUSTFile Failed."
       DISPLAY "File Status: " WS-CUSTFile-Status
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
raw.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; error handling is a plain file-status IF/ELSE, not an INVALID KEY clause (correcting the prior annotation, which claimed a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR that does not appear in the source)
raw.description: Writes the current output record to the indexed customer file; on success increments the insert counter, on any failure displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8. There is no HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program reads customer data records sequentially from a flat input file named 'customer.dat.txt'.
Each customer record read from the input file must be written to an indexed output file named 'customer.idat' sequentially.
The output indexed file uses the customer ID as the record key for indexing.
The program counts and maintains the number of records read from the input file and the number of records successfully inserted into the output file.
If a read operation on the input file fails (except end-of-file), the program terminates with an error and return code 8.
If a write operation to the output file fails, the program terminates with an error and return code 8.

complexity.justification: Sequential I/O with multi-file coordination and error handling

annotation_metadata.note: BR_004, ER_007, and ER_008 in the prior 'CORRECTED' file described a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR and a READ...NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD that do not exist in VSCBEX01.cbl; there is no INVALID KEY clause anywhere in the program and no VALIDATE_RECORD or HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph. Both rules are corrected below to reflect the actual plain IF WS-x-Good / ELSE file-status checks used throughout, and source_lines are corrected to match the real file.

rules.name: BR_001
rules.confidence_justification: Two direct OPEN statements with clear initialization intent
rules.cobol_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system initializes customer data loading by opening the source flat file for sequential input and the indexed customer file for output access, then reads the first input record.
rules.precondition: Both files are defined in FILE SECTION and not yet opened
rules.action: OPEN LOADFile for INPUT and CUSTFile for OUTPUT, then perform the priming read
rules.postcondition: Both files are open; the first LOADFile record (or EOF) is loaded

rules.name: BR_002
rules.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL construct is explicit loop control in COBOL, guarded by an explicit IF
rules.cobol_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: If the priming read succeeded, customer records are processed sequentially from the flat file in a loop that inserts the current record and reads the next one, continuing until the end-of-file condition is set.
rules.precondition: LOADFile is OPEN and the priming read (BR_001) has completed
rules.action: If the priming read was good, loop: insert current record, read next record, repeat until EOF
rules.postcondition: WS-LOADFile-EOF is TRUE; all input records have been processed

rules.name: BR_003
rules.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE statement for data transformation; clear 1:1 mapping, followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Each input customer record is copied into the indexed customer file's record structure and then written out.
rules.precondition: LOADFile-Customer-Record contains the most recently read customer data
rules.action: MOVE the customer record from input format to output format, then perform the write paragraph
rules.postcondition: CUSTFile-Customer-Record contains the customer data and has been passed to the write routine

rules.name: BR_004
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; the paragraph combines success counting with a hard-error abend path via a plain file-status IF/ELSE (no INVALID KEY clause is present in this program)
rules.cobol_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile" ...
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: Each customer record is written to the indexed customer file. If the write status is not good, the program displays diagnostics, runs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8; there is no separate INVALID KEY error path or error-handler paragraph.
rules.precondition: CUSTFile-Customer-Record is populated with data; CUSTFile is OPEN OUTPUT
rules.action: WRITE the customer record to the indexed file; on any non-good status, log and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted and the counter incremented, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: TR_005
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; unambiguous counter increment operation
rules.cobol_snippet: ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the input-file read counter by 1 after each successful record read from the flat file for audit and reconciliation purposes.
rules.precondition: FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt is initialized to zero
rules.action: Increment FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_006
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; clear counter increment
rules.cobol_snippet: ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the indexed-file insert counter by 1 after each successful record write to the customer file for reconciliation and monitoring.
rules.precondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is initialized to zero
rules.action: Increment FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_009
rules.confidence_justification: Direct DISPLAY statements; explicit output operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
rules.natural_language_rule: At the end of processing, the system displays an end-of-job banner plus the total count of records read and successfully inserted to the console for operator visibility and audit trail.
rules.precondition: Processing loop has completed; counters contain final values
rules.action: DISPLAY summary statistics to console output
rules.postcondition: Summary information is visible to operator for verification

rules.name: TR_010
rules.confidence_justification: Direct CLOSE statement; explicit resource cleanup operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
CLOSE LOADFile
      CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Both opened files (source flat file and indexed customer file) are closed before program termination to release file handles, flush buffers, and ensure data integrity.
rules.precondition: Both LOADFile and CUSTFile are OPEN
rules.action: CLOSE both files in one statement
rules.postcondition: Both files are closed; file handles and buffers released

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: ER_008
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE status check is explicit; error dispatch is a plain file-status IF/ELSE ending in abend, not an INVALID KEY clause routed to a separate error-handler paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:

   GOBACK
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When writing a record to the indexed customer file fails (duplicate key, I/O failure, or other non-good status), the program displays diagnostics, performs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8, inline in the same paragraph.
rules.precondition: A WRITE to indexed file CUSTFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on write status; on any non-good status, log diagnostics and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted successfully, or the program has terminated abnormally

      CUSTFile.

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will create and load an Indexed file with data from a flat file.
assumptions: The flat file is LINE SEQUENTIAL and the indexed file is ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will open the flat file in INPUT mode and the indexed file in OUTPUT mode.
assumptions: The files are opened before processing.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will read records from the flat file and insert them into the indexed file until the end of the flat file is reached.
assumptions: The program will only process records if the flat file read is successful.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will move the record from the flat file to the indexed file and write it.
assumptions: The record is moved from the flat file to the indexed file before writing.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will display end-of-job messages, including the number of records read and inserted.
assumptions: The end-of-job messages are displayed after processing all records.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program will handle errors during the read and write operations, and terminate the program if an error occurs.
assumptions: The program will terminate with an error code if a read or write operation fails.

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

business_rules.rule_statement: The program opens the LOADFile for input and the CUSTFile for output before processing records.
The program reads records sequentially from LOADFile until end-of-file is reached or a read error occurs.
For each successfully read record from LOADFile, the program inserts the record into the CUSTFile indexed file.
The program maintains counters for the number of records read from LOADFile and the number of records inserted into CUSTFile.
If a read error occurs on LOADFile (other than EOF), the program displays an error message, ends the job, sets return code to 8, and terminates.
If a write error occurs on CUSTFile, the program displays an error message, ends the job, sets return code to 8, and terminates.
At end of processing, the program displays end of run messages including counts of records read and inserted, then closes both files.

complexity.justification: Sequential I/O with multi-file coordination and error handling

annotation_metadata.note: BR_004, ER_007, and ER_008 in the prior 'CORRECTED' file described a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR and a READ...NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD that do not exist in VSCBEX01.cbl; there is no INVALID KEY clause anywhere in the program and no VALIDATE_RECORD or HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph. Both rules are corrected below to reflect the actual plain IF WS-x-Good / ELSE file-status checks used throughout, and source_lines are corrected to match the real file.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program opens the input file (LOADFile) in sequential mode.
The program opens the output indexed file (CUSTFile) in sequential mode.
The program reads records from the input file (LOADFile) and processes them until the end of the file is reached.
If a record is successfully read from the input file (LOADFile), the program increments the record count.

If a read operation from the input file (LOADFile) fails and the end of the file has not been reached, the program displays an error message, performs the end-of-job process, sets the return code to 8, and exits.
The program writes the customer record from the input file (LOADFile) to the output indexed file (CUSTFile).
If a write operation to the output indexed file (CUSTFile) is successful, the program increments the insert count.
If a write operation to the output indexed file (CUSTFile) fails, the program displays an error message, performs the end-of-job process, sets the return code to 8, and exits.
The program displays end-of-job messages, including the number of records read and inserted.

complexity.justification: Sequential I/O with multi-file coordination and error handling

annotation_metadata.note: BR_004, ER_007, and ER_008 in the prior 'CORRECTED' file described a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR and a READ...NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD that do not exist in VSCBEX01.cbl; there is no INVALID KEY clause anywhere in the program and no VALIDATE_RECORD or HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph. Both rules are corrected below to reflect the actual plain IF WS-x-Good / ELSE file-status checks used throughout, and source_lines are corrected to match the real file.

rules.name: BR_001
rules.confidence_justification: Two direct OPEN statements with clear initialization intent
rules.cobol_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system initializes customer data loading by opening the source flat file for sequential input and the indexed customer file for output access, then reads the first input record.
rules.precondition: Both files are defined in FILE SECTION and not yet opened
rules.action: OPEN LOADFile for INPUT and CUSTFile for OUTPUT, then perform the priming read
rules.postcondition: Both files are open; the first LOADFile record (or EOF) is loaded

rules.name: BR_002
rules.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL construct is explicit loop control in COBOL, guarded by an explicit IF
rules.cobol_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: If the priming read succeeded, customer records are processed sequentially from the flat file in a loop that inserts the current record and reads the next one, continuing until the end-of-file condition is set.
rules.precondition: LOADFile is OPEN and the priming read (BR_001) has completed
rules.action: If the priming read was good, loop: insert current record, read next record, repeat until EOF
rules.postcondition: WS-LOADFile-EOF is TRUE; all input records have been processed

rules.name: BR_003
rules.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE statement for data transformation; clear 1:1 mapping, followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Each input customer record is copied into the indexed customer file's record structure and then written out.
rules.precondition: LOADFile-Customer-Record contains the most recently read customer data
rules.action: MOVE the customer record from input format to output format, then perform the write paragraph
rules.postcondition: CUSTFile-Customer-Record contains the customer data and has been passed to the write routine

rules.name: BR_004
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE is explicit; the paragraph combines success counting with a hard-error abend path via a plain file-status IF/ELSE (no INVALID KEY clause is present in this program)
rules.cobol_snippet:
6000-Write-CUSTFile.
    WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
    ELSE
       DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile" ...
       PERFORM 3000-End-Job
       MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
       GOBACK
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: Each customer record is written to the indexed customer file. If the write status is not good, the program displays diagnostics, runs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8; there is no separate INVALID KEY error path or error-handler paragraph.
rules.precondition: CUSTFile-Customer-Record is populated with data; CUSTFile is OPEN OUTPUT
rules.action: WRITE the customer record to the indexed file; on any non-good status, log and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted and the counter incremented, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: TR_005
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; unambiguous counter increment operation
rules.cobol_snippet: ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the input-file read counter by 1 after each successful record read from the flat file for audit and reconciliation purposes.
rules.precondition: FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt is initialized to zero
rules.action: Increment FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_006
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; clear counter increment
rules.cobol_snippet: ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the indexed-file insert counter by 1 after each successful record write to the customer file for reconciliation and monitoring.
rules.precondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is initialized to zero
rules.action: Increment FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_009
rules.confidence_justification: Direct DISPLAY statements; explicit output operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
rules.natural_language_rule: At the end of processing, the system displays an end-of-job banner plus the total count of records read and successfully inserted to the console for operator visibility and audit trail.
rules.precondition: Processing loop has completed; counters contain final values
rules.action: DISPLAY summary statistics to console output
rules.postcondition: Summary information is visible to operator for verification

rules.name: TR_010
rules.confidence_justification: Direct CLOSE statement; explicit resource cleanup operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
CLOSE LOADFile
      CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Both opened files (source flat file and indexed customer file) are closed before program termination to release file handles, flush buffers, and ensure data integrity.
rules.precondition: Both LOADFile and CUSTFile are OPEN
rules.action: CLOSE both files in one statement
rules.postcondition: Both files are closed; file handles and buffers released

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: ER_008
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE status check is explicit; error dispatch is a plain file-status IF/ELSE ending in abend, not an INVALID KEY clause routed to a separate error-handler paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
ELSE
   DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile" ...
   PERFORM 3000-End-Job
   MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
   GOBACK
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When writing a record to the indexed customer file fails (duplicate key, I/O failure, or other non-good status), the program displays diagnostics, performs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8, inline in the same paragraph.
rules.precondition: A WRITE to indexed file CUSTFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on write status; on any non-good status, log diagnostics and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted successfully, or the program has terminated abnormally

business_rules.rule_statement: The program reads records sequentially from the LOADFile until end-of-file is reached.
Each record read from LOADFile is inserted into the CUSTFile indexed file.
The program counts the number of records read from LOADFile and inserted into CUSTFile.
If a read from LOADFile or a write to CUSTFile fails, the program displays an error message, performs end job procedures, sets the return code to 8, and terminates.
The program opens LOADFile in INPUT mode and CUSTFile in OUTPUT mode at the beginning of the job.
The program displays end-of-run messages including counts of records read and inserted before closing the files.

rules.cobol_snippet:

rules.name: TR_010
rules.confidence_justification: Direct CLOSE statement; explicit resource cleanup operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
CLOSE LOADFile
      CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Both opened files (source flat file and indexed customer file) are closed before program termination to release file handles, flush buffers, and ensure data integrity.
rules.precondition: Both LOADFile and CUSTFile are OPEN
rules.action: CLOSE both files in one statement
rules.postcondition: Both files are closed; file handles and buffers released

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: ER_008
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE status check is explicit; error dispatch is a plain file-status IF/ELSE ending in abend, not an INVALID KEY clause routed to a separate error-handler paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
ELSE
   DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile" ...
   PERFORM 3000-End-Job
   MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
   GOBACK
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When writing a record to the indexed customer file fails (duplicate key, I/O failure, or other non-good status), the program displays diagnostics, performs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8, inline in the same paragraph.
rules.precondition: A WRITE to indexed file CUSTFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on write status; on any non-good status, log diagnostics and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted successfully, or the program has terminated abnormally

Error messages are displayed if operations fail

business_rules.rule_statement: The program opens the input file (LOADFile) and the output indexed file (CUSTFile) for processing.
The program reads records from the input file (LOADFile) and processes them until the end of the file is reached.
For each valid record read from the input file (LOADFile), the program inserts the record into the indexed file (CUSTFile).

The program increments the record counters for the number of records read from the input file (FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt) and the number of records inserted into the indexed file (FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt).
The program displays end-of-job messages, including the total number of records read and the total number of records inserted.
The program closes the input file (LOADFile) and the indexed file (CUSTFile) at the end of processing.
The program handles file read errors by displaying an error message, performing the end-of-job process, setting the return code to 8, and terminating the program.
The program handles file write errors by displaying an error message, performing the end-of-job process, setting the return code to 8, and terminating the program.
