business_rules.rule_statement: The program reads records sequentially from the input flat file LOADFile until end-of-file is reached.
Each record read from LOADFile is moved to CUSTFile and written as a new record in the indexed output file.
The program counts the number of records read from LOADFile and the number of records successfully inserted into CUSTFile.
If a read error occurs on LOADFile (other than end-of-file), the program displays an error, ends the job, and returns with error code 8.
If a write error occurs on CUSTFile, the program displays an error, ends the job, and returns with error code 8.

complexity.justification: Sequential I/O with multi-file coordination and error handling

rules.name: BR_001
rules.confidence_justification: Two direct OPEN statements with clear initialization intent
rules.cobol_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN  INPUT LOADFile.
    OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system initializes customer data loading by opening the source flat file for sequential input and the indexed customer file for output access, then reads the first input record.
rules.precondition: Both files are defined in FILE SECTION and not yet opened
rules.action: OPEN LOADFile for INPUT and CUSTFile for OUTPUT, then perform the priming read
rules.postcondition: Both files are open; the first LOADFile record (or EOF) is loaded

rules.name: BR_002
rules.confidence_justification: PERFORM UNTIL construct is explicit loop control in COBOL, guarded by an explicit IF
rules.cobol_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-LOADFile-Good
       PERFORM UNTIL WS-LOADFile-EOF
          PERFORM 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
          PERFORM 5000-Read-LOADFile
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: If the priming read succeeded, customer records are processed sequentially from the flat file in a loop that inserts the current record and reads the next one, continuing until the end-of-file condition is set.
rules.precondition: LOADFile is OPEN and the priming read (BR_001) has completed
rules.action: If the priming read was good, loop: insert current record, read next record, repeat until EOF
rules.postcondition: WS-LOADFile-EOF is TRUE; all input records have been processed

rules.name: BR_003
rules.confidence_justification: Direct MOVE statement for data transformation; clear 1:1 mapping, followed by an explicit PERFORM of the write paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
2100-Insert-CUSTFile.
    MOVE LOADFile-Customer-Record TO
       CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
    PERFORM 6000-Write-CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Each input customer record is copied into the indexed customer file's record structure and then written out.
rules.precondition: LOADFile-Customer-Record contains the most recently read customer data
rules.action: MOVE the customer record from input format to output format, then perform the write paragraph
rules.postcondition: CUSTFile-Customer-Record contains the customer data and has been passed to the write routine

rules.action: Increment FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_006
rules.confidence_justification: Direct ADD statement; clear counter increment
rules.cobol_snippet: ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt.
rules.natural_language_rule: The system increments the indexed-file insert counter by 1 after each successful record write to the customer file for reconciliation and monitoring.
rules.precondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is initialized to zero
rules.action: Increment FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt by 1
rules.postcondition: FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt is incremented

rules.name: TR_009
rules.confidence_justification: Direct DISPLAY statements; explicit output operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
DISPLAY "      Records Read: " FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
DISPLAY "  Records Inserted: " FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
rules.natural_language_rule: At the end of processing, the system displays an end-of-job banner plus the total count of records read and successfully inserted to the console for operator visibility and audit trail.
rules.precondition: Processing loop has completed; counters contain final values
rules.action: DISPLAY summary statistics to console output
rules.postcondition: Summary information is visible to operator for verification

rules.name: TR_010
rules.confidence_justification: Direct CLOSE statement; explicit resource cleanup operation
rules.cobol_snippet:
CLOSE LOADFile
      CUSTFile.
rules.natural_language_rule: Both opened files (source flat file and indexed customer file) are closed before program termination to release file handles, flush buffers, and ensure data integrity.
rules.precondition: Both LOADFile and CUSTFile are OPEN
rules.action: CLOSE both files in one statement
rules.postcondition: Both files are closed; file handles and buffers released

rules.name: ER_007
rules.confidence_justification: READ AT END is explicit, but the surrounding IF/ELSE combines EOF handling with a hard-error abend path; there is no NOT AT END clause or validation call in this program
rules.cobol_snippet:
READ LOADFile
   AT END SET WS-LOADFile-EOF TO TRUE
END-READ.
IF WS-LOADFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt
ELSE
   IF WS-LOADFile-EOF
      NEXT SENTENCE
   ELSE
      DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-LOADFile" ...
      PERFORM 3000-End-Job
      MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
      GOBACK
   END-IF
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When a flat-file read fails for a reason other than end-of-file, the program displays diagnostic messages including the file status, runs end-of-job cleanup, sets RETURN-CODE to 8, and terminates immediately. There is no separate validation routine invoked on a successful read.
rules.precondition: A READ against LOADFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on AT END vs. genuine I/O error; on genuine error, log and abend with RETURN-CODE 8
rules.postcondition: Either the normal EOF flag is set, or the program has terminated abnormally

rules.name: ER_008
rules.confidence_justification: WRITE status check is explicit; error dispatch is a plain file-status IF/ELSE ending in abend, not an INVALID KEY clause routed to a separate error-handler paragraph
rules.cobol_snippet:
WRITE CUSTFile-Customer-Record.
IF WS-CUSTFile-Good
   ADD +1 TO FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt
ELSE
   DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 6000-Write-CUSTFile" ...
   PERFORM 3000-End-Job
   MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
   GOBACK
END-IF.
rules.natural_language_rule: When writing a record to the indexed customer file fails (duplicate key, I/O failure, or other non-good status), the program displays diagnostics, performs end-of-job cleanup, and terminates immediately with RETURN-CODE 8, inline in the same paragraph.
rules.precondition: A WRITE to indexed file CUSTFile has just been attempted
rules.action: Branch on write status; on any non-good status, log diagnostics and abend
rules.postcondition: Record is persisted successfully, or the program has terminated abnormally

business_rules.rule_statement: Open the input file (LOADFile) and the output file (CUSTFile).
Read records from the input file (LOADFile) until the end of the file is reached.
For each valid record read from the input file, insert it into the output file (CUSTFile).
If an error occurs while reading from the input file, display an error message and terminate the program.
If an error occurs while writing to the output file, display an error message and terminate the program.
At the end of the job, display the total number of records read and inserted, and close both files.

complexity.justification: Sequential I/O with multi-file coordination and error handling

annotation_metadata.note: BR_004, ER_007, and ER_008 in the prior 'CORRECTED' file described a WRITE...INVALID KEY PERFORM HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR and a READ...NOT AT END PERFORM VALIDATE_RECORD that do not exist in VSCBEX01.cbl; there is no INVALID KEY clause anywhere in the program and no VALIDATE_RECORD or HANDLE-WRITE-ERROR paragraph. Both rules are corrected below to reflect the actual plain IF WS-x-Good / ELSE file-status checks used throughout, and source_lines are corrected to match the real file.
